everyone.name: Matteo Bianchi
everyone.bio: Matteo is a seasoned Software and Cloud Engineer but also a former startup CTO, DevRel and Solution Architect. Hacker and builder, advocating for Cloud Native technologies around the world as active member of the Cloud Native community. Matteo is a Kubernetes Release Team Member and active contributor who loves Platform Engineering. Co-organizer of KCD Netherlands and CNGC Netherlands meetups, helping out different KCDs and founding organizer of Cloud Native Days. @mbianchidev on (almost) every social media.

everyone.name: Karen Chu
everyone.bio: Karen Chu is an Apple OSPO PM focused on Swift language community initiatives. Having participated in the cloud native community since 2015, she is a CNCF Ambassador, Helm community manager/maintainer, emeritus Kubernetes Code of Conduct Committee member, meet-up organizer, and conference organizer. She has also worked on The Illustrated Children’s Guide to Kubernetes book series. When she’s not connecting dots in the community, you can find her pursuing photography, cooking, and sipping on chai lattes.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Having help build this community since 2015, I'm fascinated by how far the ecosystem has come and how much further it can go. I'm extremely excited to see CNCF participate in new spaces and so with my continued work as a CNCF Ambassador, I look forward to helping bridge the gap between the cloud native and Webassembly communities.

everyone.name: Thomas Vitale
everyone.bio: Thomas Vitale is a software engineer with a focus on building cloud native solutions, currently working at Systematic in Denmark. He is the author of the "Cloud Native Spring in Action" book and plays an active role in the cloud native ecosystem as a CNCF Ambassador, Co-Chair of the CNCF Application Development Working Group, Oracle ACE Pro, and Testcontainers Community Champion.A strong advocate of open source collaboration, Thomas contributes to various projects in the Java and cloud-native space, including Spring AI, Testcontainers, Carvel, Dapr, and Buildpacks. He is also passionate about sharing his expertise with the community through talks, workshops, and writing on technical topics.

everyone.name: Hnin Yu Wai
everyone.bio: I’m passionate about cloud native technologies and Kubernetes with a strong interest in technical solution engineering.  I love exploring how systems work , breaking down complex problems , designing efficient , scalable solutions . Continuous learning and hands-on experimentation motivate me to grow in the cloud ecosystem .

everyone.name: Premkumar Selvaraj
everyone.bio: Premkumar Selvaraj is a Senior Enterprise DevOps Engineer at Elekta with a strong passion for continuous learning and admire problem solving. He is committed to enhancing his expertise in software development and driving efficiency throughout the SDLC. He advocates for automation through self-service pipelines, empowering stakeholders to navigate technical challenges while improving overall efficiency. Specialized in Azure, Cloud Infrastructure provisioning, management & monitoring, DevSecOps, Microservices architecture, GitOps, and Cybersecurity.

everyone.name: Mohan Kandasamy
everyone.bio: I am IT consultant with over 20 years of experience in UNIX/Linux space and recently started working on Cloud Native technologies.

ambassadors.name: Matteo Bianchi
ambassadors.bio: Matteo is a seasoned Software and Cloud Engineer but also a former startup CTO, DevRel and Solution Architect. Hacker and builder, advocating for Cloud Native technologies around the world as active member of the Cloud Native community. Matteo is a Kubernetes Release Team Member and active contributor who loves Platform Engineering. Co-organizer of KCD Netherlands and CNGC Netherlands meetups, helping out different KCDs and founding organizer of Cloud Native Days. @mbianchidev on (almost) every social media.

ambassadors.name: Kaslin Fields
ambassadors.bio: Kaslin Fields is a Developer Advocate at Google Cloud, CNCF Ambassador, and a contributor to Open Source Kubernetes. She is passionate about making Cloud Native technology exciting and fun to learn for everyone from seasoned pros to total newcomers. In addition to her work supporting the development of cloud native technologies as a contributor, she also aims to spread the word about this awesome field by producing talks, videos, blogs, documentation, comics, and more.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The Cloud Native ecosystem is active and growing, which can make it difficult for community members building awesome things to get the support they need. As an ambassador, I am passionate about providing support to folks building their own Cloud Native communities, technologies, and more.

ambassadors.name: Sergio Méndez
ambassadors.bio: Sergio Méndez is a DevOps Engineer at 8gears and professor of operating systems at USAC university. He is getting involved students into the CNCF Ecosystem and hosting a Cloud Native Community group and KCDs in Guatemala. He has been a speaker at several conferences such as KubeCon, WTFisCloudNative, and Kubernetes Community Days. He is also a Linkerd Ambassador and author of the book Edge Computing with Kubernetes with Packt. His website is https://sergiops.xyz.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited because I can help others to learn new technologies and getting involved to participate, also gives me the opportunity to help Latinamerican people to participate in the CNCF ecosystem and students to start contributing in open source. As an Ambassador I can reduce the gap of language creating content in Spanish.

ambassadors.name: Puja Abbassi
ambassadors.bio: Puja Abbassi is the Vice President of Product at Giant Swarm, building a managed cloud native developer platform based on Kubernetes. In Kubernetes he focuses on extending Kubernetes with custom resources and controllers. With many years of Kubernetes experience and having been in the beta batch of CKAs, he enjoys solving problems and helping people in the community, mainly within SIG Contributor Experience. He used to also be a contributor to the CIS Kubernetes Benchmarks. I blog at https://www.giantswarm.io/blog/author/puja-abbassi and sometimes on the CNCF or other community blogs.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As a CNCF ambassador, I'm passionate about bringing cloud native technologies to more developers and their organisations around the globe. Especially, as the community is growing both from a project perspective as well as on the end user side, I'm excited to bring the two closer together and see how we can enable end user organisations to build cloud native platforms using CNCF projects.Sessionize Profile: https://sessionize.com/puja

ambassadors.name: Iris Dyrmishi
ambassadors.bio: I am a Senior Observability Engineer at Miro with a strong passion for Observability and platform engineering, making these fields the center of my career. I focus on building solutions that make complex systems more transparent and reliable. Observability is more than just a skill for me; it’s a philosophy I enjoy sharing through speaking, writing, and collaborating with others. I’m also one of the organizers of Kubernetes Community Days(KCD) Porto, where I help foster a vibrant, engaged cloud-native community. Medium: https://medium.com/@irisdyrmishi

ambassadors.name: Shane Wang
ambassadors.bio: Dr. Shane Wang is an engineering director of Cloud Native China at Software and Advanced Technology Group in Intel. He and his team have been working on open source cloud software for many years since 2012, including Kubernetes, Istio, Envoy, containerd, serverless, edge, Ceph and OpenStack etc. He is an individual director of OIF foundation, alliance committee chair of SODA foundation, a TOC member of Mulan open source community, and one of the standing committee members of Open Source Development Committee in China Computer Federation (CCF), and one of Tencent Cloud Valuable Professionals. He organizes and runs cloud native meetups with China partners primarily in Shanghai and Beijing, focusing on Kubernetes, Istio and Envoy technologies.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Because with that I can organize regular meetups better in China with the local community including Tetrate, DaoCloud, Alauda, Alibaba and Tencent etc. bridging between CNCF and many China-based open source communities, I can drive more engineering contributions to CNCF projects and engagements with China customers for adoption. I'm also able to contribute more technical materials to CNCF Webinars and Blogs, etc. and pursue more presentation opportunities at CNCF conferences e.g. KubeCon China.

ambassadors.name: Rahul Jadhav
ambassadors.bio: An avid coder, a systems engineer working on solutions involving security and performance optimizations of cloud-native technologies. He runs a Kubernetes Cloud Native Security Bangalore Meetup and has been working closely with IETF Standards working groups. Taken several projects from conception to market and is an active maintainer for CNCF Sandbox project, "KubeArmor" and the lead author for IoT simulation framework "Whitefield". He co founded Accuknox where they are enabling runtime, zero-trust security by leveraging Linux-LSM/eBPF based security for k8s and containerized/cloud-native workloads.​Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Connecting to community and engaging at events, meeting new people, talking about cloud native technologies is something that I cherish. It is always great to be an ambassador of the community whose motto and modus operandi is something that I believe in. One specific goal that I would like to achieve is to establish a security focussed community locally by understanding activities that were undertaken at CNCF wide initiatives.

ambassadors.name: Simon Schrottner
ambassadors.bio: Hi, I’m Simon Schrottner, dedicated to building vibrant communities and mentoring individuals in the cloud-native ecosystem. I’m primarily focused on OpenFeature, a vendor-neutral open standard for feature flag management, where I work to drive innovation and adoption. I’m deeply committed to open source and love contributing to projects that empower developers and organizations to embrace scalable, modern solutions. I also enjoy connecting with the global tech community, sharing knowledge, and collaborating on exciting cloud-native initiatives

kubestronauts.name: Hnin Yu Wai
kubestronauts.bio: I’m passionate about cloud native technologies and Kubernetes with a strong interest in technical solution engineering.  I love exploring how systems work , breaking down complex problems , designing efficient , scalable solutions . Continuous learning and hands-on experimentation motivate me to grow in the cloud ecosystem .

kubestronauts.name: Daniel Dinu
kubestronauts.bio: More than 20 years of experience in IT. Networking and linux/UNIX system administration background. Cloud technologies enthusiast.

kubestronauts.name: Guruprasad Bhavsar
kubestronauts.bio: Senior DevOps Engineer with 9+ years of experience building and operating cloud-native platforms across financial services, SaaS, and telecom. Strong expertise in Kubernetes, GitOps, and Infrastructure as Code, backed by CNCF certifications, Gitops and recognition as a CNCF Kubestronaut (Jan 2026). Proven in delivering scalable CI/CD platforms, improving security posture, and enabling developer self-service through platform engineering.

kubestronauts.name: Rakesh Bommisetty
kubestronauts.bio: Rakesh is a Fellow Cloud Architect at Pegasystems with over 17 years of experience designing and evolving large‑scale cloud platforms. He leads innovation in cloud services, including building AI‑powered tools that enhance client autonomy and operational efficiency.  As a member of the Architecture Guild, Rakesh is responsible for solution design, cloud architecture, and enterprise‑scale transformation initiatives. He ensures architectural compliance and guides design standards across major cloud programs, with a strong focus on scalable, resilient, and cloud‑native systems.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Nourhan Mohamed
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Senior DevOps engineer and technical content creator with a passion for simplifying complex cloud-native technologies. I specialize in Kubernetes and other CNCF tools, focusing on designing hands-on learning experiences and certification content. My interests include automation, scalable infrastructure, and modern cloud-native application delivery. I also lead and mentor technical teams, driving project execution, content strategy, and skill development.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Fangel Emilio Colón Navarro
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Hello, I'm Fangel, I live in the Dominican Republic. My first steps using CNCF technologies began in 2020 when the technological world advanced significantly. As of today I have already achieved a lot of knowledge and my wishes are to continue in this wonderful world.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Ronald Lira
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I’m a Platform Engineer passionate about driving business transformation through technology. I spend my days designing new solutions, writing code, fixing bugs, coaching teammates, learning from others, and building systems that enhance performance and operational efficiency.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Phil Kildea
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I have been working in Formula 1 since 2013, starting my journey as a .NET developer. Over the years, I've transitioned into a broader technical leadership role and, at the time of writing, I serve as the Head of Development Operations. I'm passionate about building scalable systems, enabling high-performance engineering teams, and driving innovation in a fast-paced environment.
